item.title: Oracle E-Business Suite CVE-2026-46817 Actively Exploited After Patch Release
item.content:encoded:
Oracle E-Business Suite Flaw CVE-2026-46817 Actively Exploited in the Wild — Must Read

Oracle E-Business Suite flaw CVE-2026-46817 is being actively exploited against Oracle Payments systems, allowing unauthenticated attackers to potentially take over vulnerable instances. Organizations running affected versions 12.2.3 through 12.2.15 should apply Oracle’s May 2026 patches and investigate for possible compromise.

  • CVE-2026-46817 is a critical improper privilege management and authentication flaw in Oracle Payments within Oracle E-Business Suite, with a CVSS score of 9.8.
  • The flaw affects Oracle E-Business Suite versions 12.2.3 through 12.2.15, and Oracle shipped patches for it in last month’s Critical Security Patch Update.
  • Defused Cyber said it observed an actor exploiting CVE-2026-46817 on Oracle E-Business honeypots over the weekend, despite no known prior exploitation or public proof-of-concept code.
  • There are currently no details on how CVE-2026-46817 is being exploited, who is behind the activity, or whether it is part of an opportunistic or targeted campaign.
  • The article notes recent exploitation of other Oracle flaws, including CVE-2025-61882 in Oracle E-Business Suite by Cl0p-linked actors and CVE-2026-35273 in PeopleSoft Suite in ShinyHunters data theft and extortion attacks.

item.title: ShinyHunters PeopleSoft breaches, Kemp LoadMaster CVE-2026-8037, and LLM security tooling
U.S. Targets Russian Signal/WhatsApp Hackers as WhatsApp Adds Usernames, Mustang Panda Hits India
U.S. Bounties Russian Messaging Hackers as Mustang Panda and SimpleHelp Exploits Surface
Microsoft Purges StegoAd Edge Extensions as libssh2 CVE-2026-55200 PoC Emerges
KDDI Email System Breach Exposes Up to 14.2 Million ISP Logins
